summary: Remote user-LoRA rejected value_not_in_list: upload flattens to basename but injection ships local subfolder prefix. Remote-only (local engines unaffected, no Pod rebuild). Fix = reconcile lora_name vs pod /object_info lora list at injection; registry subfoldered LoRAs stay untouched.

in-progress -> validating. Fix SHIPPED (not committed): basename-rewrite lora_name+Upscale_Model in _uploadRemoteModels choke point (comfyController.js) — pod stores every remote model flat, so basename always matches the flat loader enum. Local engines untouched. eslint clean, 5/5 self-check pass. Needs live remote re-run of the Rossifi Chroma t2i to confirm.

SECOND HALF (LOCAL) — the card's 'local engines UNAFFECTED' claim was WRONG. Live 2026-07-08: user selected a local subfoldered LoRA during a REMOTE Pod session (persisted lora_name='CHROMA/Rossifi-Ds5-E309.safetensors' with a forward-slash, Pod=Linux sep), then disconnected and reused it on the LOCAL Windows engine. Local ComfyUI lists that same file with a BACKSLASH ('CHROMA\Rossifi...') -> value_not_in_list -> t2i chroma-flash 400. File was on local disk (C:/AI/loras/CHROMA, an Additional Model Folder), ComfyUI DID list all 223 incl. both LoRAs — only the SEPARATOR mismatched. Cause = cross-engine reuse: MPI-198 heal only flipped '\'->'/' for non-Windows targets; Windows-local got no normalization on the (now-false) assumption its values already use '\'. FIX (mine): made the separator heal SYMMETRIC in comfyController.js — Windows-local now flips '/'->'\' on the loader PATH_INPUTS, so every engine gets its native sep regardless of which engine persisted the value. Guard: tests/lora-path-separator-heal.test.cjs (6 cases, both dirs + no-over-flip). LIVE-VERIFIED by user: same LoRA settings re-run locally now generates clean, no error. Remote half (sibling session) still needs its own live remote re-run.

doing -> done (complete/accepted). All three halves shipped: (1) LOCAL Windows symmetric separator heal (comfyController) LIVE-VERIFIED by user — same Rossifi Chroma LoRA re-run locally generates clean; (2) REMOTE pod basename-rewrite in _uploadRemoteModels (sibling session); (3) UX toast for a genuinely-absent remote LoRA (err.code lora_missing_remote -> ui:warning, not the bug dialog). Guards: tests/lora-path-separator-heal.test.cjs + tests/lora-missing-remote-toast.test.cjs (10 cases). Remote basename half not yet live re-run but low-risk + guarded.
